mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 6.0 percent, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, depressing equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in real economic activity, triggering a risk-off repricing that suppresses equity valuations over the following month.
failure_condition: The joint decline signal fails to predict negative forward returns in more than 55% of out-of-sample instances, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows a p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid capacity expansion, surging industrial output outpaces demand growth, leading to inventory gluts and margin compression that drag down equity returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: The signal predicts negative returns in less than 40% of instances during the specified regime, or forward returns show no statistically significant difference from the unconditional mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth expands corporate profit margins and boosts earnings expectations, driving positive equity returns over the subsequent month as investors price in stronger fundamentals.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index signals rising inflation expectations that erode real discount rates and trigger central bank tightening fears, suppressing equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal predicts negative returns in less than 45% of triggered instances, or forward returns show no statistically significant difference from the unconditional mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rapid growth in total nonfarm payrolls combined with stable unemployment indicates a robust labor market that supports consumer demand and corporate earnings, driving positive equity returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive forward returns in more than 55% of out-of-sample instances, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows a p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Rising residential mortgage debt levels reflect increased household leverage and consumption capacity, which supports corporate revenues and drives positive equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in credit expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
